diagrams.title: CPU vs GPU Architecture Comparison
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison of CPU and GPU chip layouts showing core count, cache hierarchy, and control complexity
elements: CPU: Few complex cores with large L3 cache
GPU: Thousands of simple cores with small caches
Control Unit: Complex in CPU, simple in GPU
Memory: CPU has uniform access, GPU has layered memory
diagrams.purpose: Understand fundamental architectural differences between processors optimized for different workloads

diagrams.title: CUDA Thread Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Hierarchical organization of GPU threads from grid to individual thread
elements: Grid → Block (max 1024 threads) → Warp (32 threads) → Thread
Each level has unique ID: gridDim, blockIdx, blockDim, threadIdx
Blocks run on SMs, threads in warp execute together
Shared memory per block, registers per thread
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how threads are organized and mapped to GPU hardware for parallel execution

diagrams.title: GPU Memory Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Pyramid showing GPU memory types from fastest to slowest with capacity and characteristics
elements: Shared Memory: 16-48 KB per SM, fast
diagrams.purpose: Understand memory access patterns and optimization strategies for GPU code
